explanation: This LTL captures the requirement that the robot must provide a detailed explanation of its current location and the completion status of the current mission. It ensures that at any point in time (G), the robot must not only inform users of its location (robotLocation) but also state whether the task (mission) is complete or not (missioncomplete).

This LTL states that the robot must explain its location and indicate whether it has completed the mission at each time step. It requires the robot to provide its location (robotLocation) and confirm the status of task completion (missioncomplete) whenever it is detailed. It is an aggregation that insists on both pieces of information being available continuously as long as detailed information is being requested.

This LTL asserts that the robot provides its current location and the status of task completion continuously throughout its operation. This ensures that both elements are communicated and confirms that the task (as represented by mission) can either be in progress or complete states, responding to the location queries.

This formula indicates that anytime the robot provides an input regarding its location, it will also eventually indicate the potential for task completion. This captures the essence of providing a location-based explanation while ensuring it states whether a task can be completed or not.

This formula asserts that if the robot's current location is provided, it is guaranteed to eventually explain whether it can complete its current task or not. This captures both the location explanation and task completion status which are key elements of the request.

Here, the formula guarantees that whenever the robot communicates its location, it will eventually provide a status update indicating if the mission can be completed. This guarantees two major aspects: the location and the completion of the task.

This LTL property ensures that if the robot gives information about its location, it will timely provide an assessment of its task completion status. This captures the requirement to continuously inform about its state based on location.

This expression specifies that whenever the robot indicates its position, it will provide an explanation about either the path it follows or about completion of the task afterwards. This connects both tasks of location explanation and task review.

This property conveys that whenever the robot asserts its location, it will deliver a conclusion about its task performance thereafter. This aligns with relaying its current situation concerning task completion.

This formula quantifies that every time the robot shares its location, it also engages in explaining the path it has undertaken or intends to undertake until the task is completed. The response meets both criteria effectively.

This LTL ensures that when the robot provides its location, it will eventually justify the options available for task completion. This captures the location and the explanation about available actions smoothly.

This property ensures that should the robot indicate its position, it will subsequently provide justification regarding its task completion probabilities. This forms a comprehensive response involving location and task progress.

The formula guarantees that revealing the robot's location necessitates a follow-up with an explanation of the self-recovery strategy should complications arise. This emphasizes the dual aspect of location confirmation and readiness to clarify operations.

This LTL property specifies that whenever the robot's location is verified, it can provide a detailed explanation until it completes its mission. This captures the requirement to both explain the location and the task's completion status effectively, ensuring that the robot communicates its progress and whereabouts systematically.

This property denotes that following the robot's location being stated, it must provide an explanation regarding its actions until the mission reaches completion. This ensures that users are informed about both the current position of the robot and whether it is successful in completing its tasks, fulfilling the purpose conveyed in the natural language input.

The LTL formula captures that upon returning the current location, the robot provides explanations pertinent to its actions until the mission is complete or successfully finished. This succinctly integrates the elements of task location and completion status, ensuring that they are addressed in a seamless manner during operation.

This property indicates that whenever the robot communicates its location, it guarantees to explain its current path and eventual task completion status. It ensures that location and task progress are both transparently communicated to the user throughout the execution of the mission.

This LTL criteria expresses that after providing its location, the robot is required to explain the actions taken until it either reaches a completion state regarding its mission or experiences a change in the planned task. This ensures consistent communication of progress linked with location.

This formula ensures that every time the robot states its location, it must justify its actions until the mission is manifestly completed. This ties together the aspects of providing location context and validating action outcomes into a coherent and informative framework for users.

The property ensures that each time the robot acknowledges its location, it must perform a situation assessment consistently until it successfully completes the mission. This captures the necessity of contextual operational updates along with task progress assessments during mission execution.

This formulation ensures whenever the robot articulates its location, it must communicate alternative action paths until the mission is finalized. This enriches the information provided by outlining the current location and possible paths forward while confirming completion status.

The formula captures that when the robot mentions its current location, it must deliver explanations on health status until the mission's completion. This links the location reporting directly with the important aspect of the robot's condition through the mission lifecycle, ensuring a comprehensive explanation is always available.

This LTL property encapsulates that whenever the robot reports its location, it undertakes diagnostic measures and provides an update until the mission is completed. This ensures continual assessment while mapping out the operational flow, maintaining clarity on both the robot’s status and task fulfilment.

The LTL formula captures the requirement that while the robot is at its current location, it will continuously provide detailed explanations about its task completion status until the mission is marked as complete. This ensures the user receives ongoing updates regarding the task's progress while at the specific location.

This formula ensures that whenever the robot is at its current location, it must explain its location every time until the mission is complete. This guarantees that the user is informed about not only the robot's location but also about the task's eventual completion.

This LTL property specifies that if a safety concern is detected, the robot will explain its current location, and will do so until the mission reaches completion. This is crucial for maintaining safety standards while ensuring the user is informed about the actions taken.

This captures the requirement that if the robot has detected an unexpected condition, it must provide an explanation of its current location and task status until the mission is completed. This is important for transparency during unexpected issues.

This LTL formula states that while the robot continues with its mission, it will explain its location and provide task completion updates until the mission is complete. This ensures ongoing communication about progress and actions taken.

This formula mandates that if the robot encounters an unexpected obstacle, it must explain why it is at its current location until the mission is completed. This fosters user understanding during complications.

In this formula, whenever the robot is following a path, it must provide an explanation of its location and whether the task can be completed until it completes the mission. This allows for tracking of both location and task viability throughout the operation.
Here we ensure that while the robot is manipulating objects, it provides clarity on its location and the task's progress until mission completion, reinforcing accountability during such operations.

This captures the essence of a detailed explanation about the robot's current location and whether the task is complete, explicitly until the mission has been fulfilled. It focuses on transparency and informs users about the task status during operation.

This LTL asserts that if the robot is in a performance review mode, it must continuously explain its location and task completion status until the mission is complete. This aligns with quality assurance protocols during evaluations and reviews.

This LTL property ensures that whenever the robot's location is returned, it must also provide a justification or reasoning for that location until the mission is complete. This captures the essence of providing location context while confirming task completion.

This LTL statement guarantees that when an unexpected condition is detected, the robot must explain its current path until the completion of the mission. This fulfills the requirement of location context as well as monitoring the task’s completion status effectively.

This property ensures that every time the robot detects a failure, it should provide an explanation about the current task’s probability of completion until the mission is completed. This format enriches the understanding of status and progress toward mission fulfillment.

This ensures that whenever the system enters a training mode, it will provide details about its current location until the mission is marked as complete. It caters the need to clarify location and ongoing task context.

This property mandates that as long as the robot is engaged in path planning, it must explain the intended path until the mission is accomplished. It addresses the requirement for clarity about location and ongoing activities.

This statement guarantees that whenever a safety concern is detected, the robot must provide detailed explanations regarding its current status until the mission is concluded. It effectively combines safety awareness with task completion.

This captures the requirement such that when the robot successfully places an object, it should explain that action until the mission is completed. This combines task accomplishment with context about actions taken.

Whenever an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot is required to explain its recovery strategy until the mission concludes. This focuses on communication regarding obstacles and strategic recovery, supporting the overall task context.

This property ensures that if any deviations from the planned route are noted, the robot must return to informing about its current path until the mission is complete. It emphasizes adherence to the planned execution while confirming location context.

This LTL statement mandates that any time the robot initiates a performance review, it must provide a detailed account of its current status until the mission is accomplished. This links ongoing activities with performance evaluations effectively.

When a situation assessment is performed, the robot must explain which locations it has visited until the task completion status is reached. This meets the requirement of evaluating the situation while ensuring the context of visited locations is clear.

This LTL property ensures that whenever there is a need for a health update, the robot must provide an explanation of its health status until the task is completed, addressing both health monitoring and task fulfillment.

This property mandates that if the robot encounters a problem, it should inform about the locations it has visited until the mission status is reached. This emphasizes clarity on problem context intertwined with location history.

This statement captures the requirement for when the output is requested, the robot must provide alternatives for task completion until the mission is achieved. This diversifies the robot's responses while aligning with the ongoing mission context.

This ensures that any unexpected behavior must be followed by an explanation of the alternatives until the mission completion is confirmed. It highlights the robot’s adaptability in uncertain situations with clarity in actions.

This property enforces that when the robot is in manipulation mode, it must provide information about the objects it is handling until the mission is accomplished. This supports an understanding of the robot’s activity and task progression.

This LTL property mandates that if a time constraint is present, the robot must justify its action until the mission completion occurs. It ensures transparency regarding time-sensitive decisions in relation to task fulfillment.
